skill.name: playwright-cli
skill.description: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction using Playwright CLI.
skill.summary: Browser automation skill for web testing, screenshot capture, form filling, and data extraction via Playwright CLI commands.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 289 patterns (187 external_commands, 51 network, 1 filesystem) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are markdown documentation examples, not actual code execution. Network findings are URLs in documentation for browser navigation examples. Browser credential/storage access is intentional functionality for testing authenticated workflows. This is a legitimate Microsoft Playwright CLI wrapper skill with appropriate tool scoping via allowed-tools directive.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution Capability
low_findings.description: Skill can execute arbitrary playwright-cli commands via Bash. This is by design but requires user trust in command outputs.

content.user_title: Automate browser testing and web interactions
content.value_statement: Manual web testing and repetitive browser tasks consume significant development time. This skill automates browser interactions through Playwright CLI for efficient testing, screenshots, form filling, and data extraction.
actual_capabilities: Navigate browsers and interact with web pages using CLI commands
Take screenshots and PDF captures of web pages or specific elements
Fill forms, click elements, and simulate user interactions
Manage browser sessions with isolated cookies and storage
Execute custom Playwright code for advanced automation scenarios
Save and load browser state for authenticated workflow testing
limitations: Requires Playwright CLI to be installed on the system
Browser automation runs in the local environment with access to installed browsers
Cannot automate mobile browsers or native mobile applications
Persistent browser profiles require explicit --persistent flag

use_cases.title: End-to-End Web Testing
use_cases.description: Automate complete user workflows like login, navigation, form submission, and verification across multiple pages.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers and developers testing web applications

use_cases.title: Automated Screenshot Capture
use_cases.description: Capture screenshots of web pages, specific elements, or full-page captures for documentation or visual regression testing.
use_cases.target_user: Technical writers and designers documenting UI changes

use_cases.title: Web Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Scrape data from websites with complex interactions including pagination, dynamic content, and authenticated access.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts gathering information from web sources

prompt_templates.title: Navigate and screenshot a webpage
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a browser and navigate to https://example.com, then take a screenshot of the full page.

prompt_templates.title: Fill and submit a form
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to the login page at https://example.com/login, fill the email field with user@example.com, fill the password field, click the submit button, and capture the result.

prompt_templates.title: Test authenticated workflow with saved state
prompt_templates.prompt: Save the current browser state to auth.json after logging in. Later, load the saved state to continue testing without re-authenticating.

prompt_templates.title: Execute custom Playwright automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run custom Playwright code to grant geolocation permissions, set the location to coordinates 37.7749, -122.4194, then navigate to a map service and verify the location is applied.

output_examples.input: Navigate to https://playwright.dev and take a screenshot
output_examples.output: Browser opened successfully. Navigated to https://playwright.dev. Page title: Fast and reliable end-to-end testing for modern web apps. Screenshot saved to page-2026-03-24T08-30-00-000Z.png
Filled email field with user@example.com. Filled password field. Clicked submit button. Navigation detected to /dashboard. Page title: User Dashboard. Login successful.
Browser state saved to auth.json. Contains 3 cookies, 5 localStorage entries, and 2 sessionStorage entries. State can be restored with state-load auth.json command.

best_practices: Use named browser sessions (-s flag) to isolate different workflows and prevent cookie conflicts
Always close browser sessions with close or close-all commands to free resources
Save authentication state with state-save after login to reuse in subsequent test runs
anti_patterns: Do not use kill-all in production workflows as it forcefully terminates all browser processes
Avoid using --persistent without explicit need, as it writes browser data to disk
Do not hardcode credentials in commands, use state-save/state-load for authenticated workflows

faq.question: How do I install Playwright CLI?
faq.answer: Install via npm with: npm install -g @playwright/cli. Alternatively, use npx playwright-cli to run without global installation.

faq.question: How do I run multiple browser sessions at once?
faq.answer: Use the -s flag to create named sessions. Example: playwright-cli -s=session1 open site1.com and playwright-cli -s=session2 open site2.com for isolated concurrent sessions.

faq.question: How can I test authenticated workflows without logging in each time?
faq.answer: Use state-save to save browser state after login, then use state-load to restore the saved state in future sessions.

faq.question: What browsers are supported?
faq.answer: Playwright CLI supports Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, Chrome, and Edge. Specify with --browser=firefox or --browser=msedge flags.

faq.question: How do I take a screenshot of a specific element?
faq.answer: Use the snapshot command to get element references, then run: playwright-cli screenshot element-id --filename=element.png

faq.question: Can I automate mobile viewports?
faq.answer: Yes, use the resize command to set viewport dimensions: playwright-cli resize 375 667 for iPhone SE dimensions, or configure via config file.
